revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:生根神社 (大阪市西成区)]][[zh:生根神社 (大阪市西成区)]] {{Infobox religious building| |image=File:Ikune jinja (Nishinari, Osaka) haiden2.jpg |name=Ikune Shrine |location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} {{ill|Nishinari-ku, Osaka|en|Nishinari-ku, Osaka|qid=Q985695|lt=Nishinari Ward|1=Nishinari-ku, Osaka}} {{ill|Tamade|ja|玉出|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamade|1=Tamade|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572405}} 2-1-10, Tamade-nishi, Nishinari-ku, Osaka |latitude_degrees=34 |latitude_minutes=37 |latitude_seconds=33.5 |latNS=N |longitude_degrees=135 |longitude_minutes=29 |longitude_seconds=24.5 |lonEW=E |map_country_code=JP |map=Osaka city |enshrined_deities={{ill|Sukunahikona-no-mikoto|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona-no-mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
{{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭児命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1067028}}
{{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane公|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} |annual_festival={{ill|October 9|en|October 9|qid=Q2923|lt=October 9|1=October 9}} |ritual={{ill|Kotsuma Nankin Festival|ja|こつまなんきん祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuma Nankin Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Winter solstice|en|Winter solstice|qid=Q207932|lt=Winter Solstice|1=Winter solstice}}) |prefecture={{ill|Q102412467|qid=Q102412467|1=Q102412467}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.62597222|N|135.49013889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Hiruko|qid=Q1067028|1=Hiruko}}; {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qid=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}} }} '''Ikune Shrine''' (生根神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|qid=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} {{ill|Osaka|en|Osaka|qid=Q35765|lt=Osaka City|1=Osaka}} {{ill|Nishinari-ku, Osaka|en|Nishinari-ku, Osaka|qid=Q985695|lt=Nishinari Ward|1=Nishinari-ku, Osaka}} {{ill|Tamade|ja|玉出|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamade|1=Tamade|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572405}}. == History == The date of its establishment is unknown, but the area was once part of the sacred territory of {{ill|Sumiyoshi-taisha|en|Sumiyoshi-taisha|qid=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}}. It is said that a branch shrine of {{ill|Ikune Shrine (Osaka City's Sumiyoshi Ward)|ja|生根神社 (大阪市住吉区)|lt=Ikune Shrine (Osaka City's Sumiyoshi Ward)|lt_ja=生根神社 (大阪市住吉区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikune Shrine (Osaka City's Sumiyoshi Ward)|1=Ikune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574575}}, which originally featured an inner {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|qid=Q798838|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenmangū}} (currently independent from Sumiyoshi Taisha), was established. From this shrine, the branch spirit of {{ill|Sukunabikona|en|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukunabikona}} was enshrined as the {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|qid=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} of {{ill|Tamade|ja|玉出|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamade|1=Tamade|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572405}}. There is also a tradition that worship of {{ill|Hiruko|ja|ヒルコ|lt=Hiruko|lt_ja=蛭児命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiruko|1=Hiruko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1067028}} had been practiced even earlier. It is said that the sacred object of {{ill|Nishinomiya Shrine|en|Nishinomiya Shrine|qid=Q705297|lt=Nishinomiya Shrine|1=Nishinomiya Shrine}}, which was washed ashore by a flood, was enshrined here, and even after the sacred object ({{ill|Shintai|en|Shintai|qid=Q327532|lt=Shintai|1=Shintai}}) was returned to Nishinomiya Shrine, a branch spirit continued to be venerated, marking the founding of this shrine. In the early Meiji period, the {{ill|Chikuzen Tenmangu|ja|筑前天満宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Tenmangu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579217|1=Chikuzen Tenmangu}}—formerly enshrined at the Chikuzen residence in Osaka—was incorporated. [[ja:生根神社 (大阪市西成区)]] == Daigaku == [[File:生根神社だいがく.jpg|thumb|The "Daigaku" of Ikune Shrine]] At Ikune Shrine, the '''Daigaku''' is a long-held tradition. Written in kanji as “台楽” or “台額”, the daigaku is a tower—approximately 20 meters tall and adorned with about 70 lanterns—that has been used in rainmaking rituals since ancient times. It is said that in the past, shrines around southern Osaka also featured a daigaku. However, all such structures except the one at Ikune Shrine were destroyed by war damage during {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|qid=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}, with only the one at Ikune Shrine surviving due to evacuation. This daigaku was designated as a tangible cultural property of Osaka Prefecture in March 1972, and to commemorate this designation, both a central daigaku and a female daigaku were newly constructed[http://osaka.thepage.jp/detail/20150729-00000001-wordleafv　女子も奮闘 大阪・生根神社　夜空を焦がす巨大「だいがく」]THE PAGE, 2015.07.29. Currently, it is open to the public only for two days during the summer festival held from July 24 to 25. At the festival, {{ill|Hamamura Jun|ja|浜村淳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamura Jun|1=Jun Hamamura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557717}} participates every year, and in 2010 a stone monument was erected bearing the haiku “Come, sing along to the daigaku dance tune, summer moon.” == Kotsuma Pumpkin Mound == The old name of the area, {{ill|Katsuma (Kotsuma) Village|ja|玉出町|lt=Katsuma (Kotsuma) Village|lt_ja=勝間（こつま）村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuma (Kotsuma) Village|1=Tamade|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11572409}}, is commemorated by a mound celebrating the local specialty of small, dark, and richly flavored pumpkins. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}} in {{ill|Osaka|ja|大坂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osaka|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大阪|1=Ōsaka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11439906}}, these pumpkins were famed as one of the signature {{ill|Naniwa Vegetables|ja|なにわ野菜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Vegetables|1=naniwayasai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11274449}}—alongside those from {{ill|Tennoji|ja|天王寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tennoji|1=Tennōji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10940450}} and {{ill|Turnip|ja|蕪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Turnip|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カブ|1=Turnip|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3916957}}. Katsuma merchants peddled these goods to Osaka via the {{ill|Katsuma Kaidō|ja|勝間街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuma Kaidō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and the pumpkins were reputedly eaten on the {{ill|Winter solstice|en|Winter solstice|qid=Q207932|lt=Winter Solstice|1=Winter solstice}} as a remedy against {{ill|Stroke|ja|中風|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stroke|1=Cerebral apoplexy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10876841}} and colds. At this shrine, a ritual known as the {{ill|Kotsuma Nankin Festival|ja|こつまなんきん祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuma Nankin Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is held on the winter solstice, during which steamed pumpkin ({{ill|Kotsuma Pumpkin Steamed|ja|こつま南瓜蒸し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuma Pumpkin Steamed|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) is served to visitors, who partake in it to ward off stroke, prevent senility, and ensure good health. On that day, candies made from traditional Naniwa vegetables and shochu produced from Kotsuma pumpkin are also sold. Furthermore, the term “kotsuma nankin” is also used as an expression of endearment for women who, like young {{ill|Ama (diving)|en|Ama (diving)|qid=Q1577970|lt=Ama|1=Ama (diving)}}, are noted for their dark complexions, petite and well-toned figures, and finely textured skin—a compliment even extolled in boudoir settings. In addition, {{ill|Kondō Kō|ja|今東光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kondō Kō|1=Tōkō Kon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1759101}}’s work {{ill|Kotsuma Nankin|ja|こつまなんきん|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuma Nankin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, which also inspired the films “Kotsuma Nankin” and “Zoku Kotsuma Nankin” starring {{ill|Sagami Sachiko|ja|瑳峨三智子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sagami Sachiko|1=Michiko Saga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574004}}, depicts such women. The name Katsuma is considered to share the same meaning as that in {{ill|Motoori Norinaga|en|Motoori Norinaga|qid=Q384647|lt=Motoori Norinaga|1=Motoori Norinaga}}, as exemplified by {{ill|Tamakattsuma|ja|玉勝間|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamakattsuma|1=Tamakatsuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55530290}}—encompassing notions of {{ill|Basket|en|Basket|qid=Q201097|lt=Basket|1=Basket}}, {{ill|Kagome Kagome|en|Kagome Kagome|qid=Q1401167|lt=Kagome Kagome|1=Kagome Kagome}}, {{ill|Watercraft|en|Watercraft|qid=Q1229765|lt=Boat|1=Watercraft}}, and {{ill|Net (textile)|en|Net (textile)|qid=Q504825|lt=Net|1=Net (textile)}}—reflecting the area’s ancient coastal character. In the legend of the nearby {{ill|Abe no Oji Shrine|ja|阿倍王子神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Oji Shrine|1=Abe Ōji Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11657249}}, the location is described with the phrase “the edge of Tamakattsuma” (玉勝間の{{ill|Gen|ja|艮|1=Gèn (Bagua)|2=ja|3=艮|lt=Gen (艮)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gen|qq=}}). The positional relationship between Abe no Oji Shrine and Katsuma Village has also been intriguingly compared to the pattern of a seaman’s star ({{ill|Pentagram|en|Pentagram|qid=Q182186|lt=Pentagram|1=Pentagram}}, associated with {{ill|Abe no Seimei|en|Abe no Seimei|qid=Q1150151|lt=Abe no Seimei|1=Abe no Seimei}}) and a basket (linked to {{ill|Ashiya Dōman|ja|蘆屋道満|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiya Dōman|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道満|1=Ashiya Dōman|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11641735}}), while another theory attributes the term to the shamanistic {{ill|Magic|en|Magic|qid=Q224102|lt=magic|1=Magic}} of the {{ill|Ama (diving)|en|Ama (diving)|qid=Q1577970|lt=Ama diver|1=Ama (diving)}} people. == Access == * Approximately 300 meters north of {{ill|Tamade Station (Osaka)|en|Tamade Station (Osaka)|qid=Q125822|lt=Tamade Station|1=Tamade Station (Osaka)}} on the {{ill|Yotsubashi Line|en|Yotsubashi Line|qid=Q1067748|lt=Osaka Metro Yotsubashi Line|1=Yotsubashi Line}}. * Approximately 400 meters west of {{ill|Kishinosato-Tamade Station|en|Kishinosato-Tamade Station|qid=Q6416603|lt=Kishiri-Tamade Station|1=Kishinosato-Tamade Station}} on the {{ill|Nankai Main Line|en|Nankai Main Line|qid=Q59453|lt=Nankai Main Line|1=Nankai Main Line}} and {{ill|Koya Line|en|Koya Line|qid=Q71986|lt=Nankai Koya Line|1=Koya Line}}. == References == == See Also == * {{ill|Ikune Shrine (Osaka City's Sumiyoshi Ward)|ja|生根神社 (大阪市住吉区)|lt=Ikune Shrine (Osaka City's Sumiyoshi Ward)|lt_ja=生根神社 (大阪市住吉区)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikune Shrine (Osaka City's Sumiyoshi Ward)|1=Ikune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11574575}} (in {{ill|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|en|Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka|qid=Q985702|lt=Sumiyoshi Ward|1=Sumiyoshi-ku, Osaka}}, Osaka City) – the original source of the branch spirit ({{ill|Branch Spirit Invitation|ja|分霊#勧請|lt=Invitation|lt_ja=勧請|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Branch Spirit Invitation|1=Bunrei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q195793}}). * {{ill|Yakuso|ja|薬祖神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakuso|1=Three Medicine Gods|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11622504}} == External Links == * [http://www.ikune.net/index.php?top=1&top3=1&moji=1 Kamino Tenjin Ikune Shrine] - Official Website * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai8sibu/nisinari-ku/m01x_01_nisinari_ikune.html Ikune Shrine] - Osaka Prefecture Shrine Agency * [http://www.osaka-info.jp/jp/events/festivals_events/33.html Daigaku Festival] - OSAKA-INFO Osaka Tourism Information (Osaka Convention & Tourism Bureau) {{commons|Category:Ikune Shrine (Nishinari, Osaka)}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|生根神社|version=39231635|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:生根神社]] [[ja:生根神社 (大阪市西成区)]] {{translated page|ja|生根神社 (大阪市西成区)|version=102052755|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Ikune jinja (Nishinari, Osaka)}} [[zh:生根神社 (大阪市西成区)]] {{wikidata link|Q11574576}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Nishinari-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Nishinari-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:Osaka Historical Sites Connecting Walkway]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
